laws.title: Massachusetts Minimum Wage — $15.00/hr (2023 and forward)
laws.summary: Massachusetts' minimum wage is $15.00/hour. Tipped employees: $6.75/hr (with tip credit). Service employees: $6.75/hr. Massachusetts has a separate 'Blue Laws' overtime provision for retail on Sundays and holidays — employees must be paid premium pay (currently being phased out as of Jan 2023, with full phase-out by 2023).
compliance_actions: Confirm all Massachusetts employees are paid at least $15.00/hr
Note: Sunday/holiday premium pay requirements for retail have been phased out as of January 2023
Ensure tipped employees meet the $15.00/hr floor when tips are combined with the base rate

laws.title: Massachusetts PFML — Paid Family & Medical Leave
laws.summary: Massachusetts Paid Family and Medical Leave provides up to 26 weeks of paid family leave and 20 weeks of paid medical leave. Employers with 25+ MA employees contribute to the state fund. Small employers (under 25 MA employees) are not required to contribute the employer share but must withhold the employee share. Benefits can run concurrently with FMLA.
compliance_actions: Verify you are registered with the Massachusetts PFML program
Withhold and remit employee and employer contributions (check current rates at mass.gov)
Post required PFML notices at all Massachusetts worksites
Update your leave policy to address how PFML coordinates with FMLA and any company paid leave

laws.title: Massachusetts Pay Equity Act — Equal Pay Requirements
laws.summary: The Massachusetts Equal Pay Act prohibits wage discrimination based on gender for 'comparable work' (a broader standard than federal 'equal work'). Employers cannot ask for salary history from applicants. Employers who conduct self-evaluations and act in good faith to correct disparities may have an affirmative defense. All employers covered regardless of size.
compliance_actions: Remove salary history questions from job applications and interview processes
Conduct a pay equity analysis for Massachusetts employees to identify disparities
Document your pay equity review to establish the good-faith affirmative defense
Ensure job descriptions reflect comparable work accurately
